name: Koa (Next-Generation Express Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); TJ Holowaychuk / Koa team

auth.notes: Web framework; integrate any auth middleware

performance.notes: Lighter than Express; no unnecessary overhead; slower than Fastify/Hono

agent_readiness.notes: Minimalist Node.js web framework by Express creators. Uses async/await natively via ctx (context) object. Middleware onion model. ctx.request, ctx.response, ctx.state. No routing/body parsing/etc. built in (add @koa/router, koa-body). Elegant error handling with try/catch across middleware. Smaller than Express. More modern than Express (no callback hell). Less ecosystem than Express. Fastify and Hono have superseded it for new projects wanting performance.

gotchas: Bare minimum — need separate packages for routing, body parsing, etc.
New projects better served by Fastify or Hono
best_when: Teams already using Koa who need to maintain existing apps
Simple Node.js APIs preferring async/await over Express callbacks
avoid_when: New Node.js projects (Fastify or Hono better choices)
summary: Koa is a minimalist async/await Node.js framework by the Express creators. ctx object, onion-model middleware, elegant error handling. No built-ins — add @koa/router, koa-body etc. Smaller/simpler than Express. More modern than Express but superseded by Fastify (performance) and Hono (edge). Best for maintaining existing Koa apps. New projects should use Fastify or Hono.
